action.reasoning: Observation: Best test PESQ still 3.137 (MetricGAN+ w/ -25 LUFS, val flat at 2.862 across postprocs). ConvTasNet after 10 epochs reaches val PESQ~2.02 (train loss -14.2) but test_enhance.py crashed on torch.load of best.pt due to numpy.core.multiarray.scalar not allowed in weights_only=True (default in PyTorch 2.6); checkpoint likely contains optimizer/scheduler state with numpy scalars. train.py is functional but incomplete in view (full code ran previously). Analysis: MetricGAN+ (task-specific, PESQ-optimized) remains superior; ConvTasNet undertrained (only slight improvement over noisy baseline ~1.9). Root cause of load error is safe unpickling - fix via add_safe_globals. Hybrid (ConvTasNet pre-filter + MetricGAN) or longer ConvTasNet training (lower LR=5e-4, more epochs=30, better loss balance, full val=100) could help but risks time. Decision: Rewrite test_enhance.py (add safe_globals for numpy scalar, load both models, eval ConvTasNet w/ loudness norm on 100 val samples for fair comparison, test hybrid if time allows). Run it to diagnose ConvTasNet val PESQ w/ postproc. If >2.86 switch predict.py to ConvTasNet+norm or hybrid and submit_eval; else restore MetricGAN submit and plan longer ConvTasNet training in next iters (scale based on ~200s/iter).
